title: Linux Essentials
description: Question batch for the Networking Fundamentals topic.

questions.question: What is an IP address used for?
answers: Identifying a device on a network
questions.explanation: An IP address identifies a host or interface on a network.

questions.question: Which command often tests network reachability to another host?
questions.explanation: ping sends ICMP echo requests to test reachability.

questions.question: Which service translates hostnames into IP addresses?
questions.explanation: DNS resolves names to IP addresses and vice versa.

questions.question: Which protocol is commonly used for secure remote shell access?
questions.explanation: SSH provides encrypted remote access to shell sessions.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a subnet mask?
answers: To define the network and host portion of an IP address
questions.explanation: A subnet mask determines which bits identify the network and which identify hosts.

questions.question: Which port is commonly associated with SSH?
questions.explanation: SSH commonly listens on TCP port 22.

questions.question: Which command commonly displays network interface configuration on many Linux systems?
questions.explanation: The ip command is widely used to inspect and manage interfaces and routes.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a default gateway?
answers: To route traffic to other networks
questions.explanation: A default gateway forwards traffic destined خارج the local network.

questions.question: Which protocol automatically assigns IP addresses on many networks?
questions.explanation: DHCP dynamically assigns IP configuration to clients.

questions.question: Which service keeps system clocks synchronized over networks?
questions.explanation: NTP synchronizes time between systems.

questions.question: Which command can show active listening ports and sockets on Linux?
questions.explanation: ss displays socket and connection information.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of SSH over Telnet?
questions.explanation: SSH protects credentials and session data with encryption.

questions.question: Which file transfer tool commonly uses SSH for secure copying?
questions.explanation: scp copies files securely over SSH.

questions.question: What is a hostname?
answers: A human-readable name for a system
questions.explanation: A hostname is the name used to identify a system on a network.

questions.question: Which command commonly shows the route traffic will take through network devices?
questions.explanation: traceroute shows the hops between the source and a destination.

questions.question: Which protocol is used for web browsing without encryption?
questions.explanation: HTTP is the unencrypted web protocol.

questions.question: Which protocol is used for encrypted web browsing?
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses TLS to protect web traffic.

questions.question: Why is DNS important on Linux systems?
answers: It helps systems locate services by name
questions.explanation: DNS allows systems and users to use names instead of numeric IP addresses.

questions.question: What is the purpose of `/etc/hosts`?
questions.explanation: The /etc/hosts file provides local static hostname resolution.

questions.question: Which issue is most likely if DNS name resolution fails but IP connectivity works?
answers: Hostnames may not resolve even though the network is reachable
questions.explanation: DNS failures can affect hostname-based access while basic IP connectivity still works.

questions.question: Why is time synchronization important for Linux servers?
answers: It improves log correlation and security troubleshooting
questions.explanation: Accurate time helps with logs, authentication, and troubleshooting.

questions.question: Which check is most useful when an SSH connection fails immediately?
answers: Verify host reachability and listening service status
questions.explanation: A quick network and service check often finds the immediate cause.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a firewall on a Linux host?
questions.explanation: A firewall permits or blocks traffic based on configured rules.

questions.question: Which command is commonly used to retrieve a web resource from the command line?
questions.explanation: curl is widely used to request URLs from the command line.

questions.question: Why is port knowledge useful in troubleshooting?
answers: It helps identify which service should be listening
questions.explanation: Knowing standard ports helps verify whether expected services are available.

questions.question: Which approach best supports safe remote administration of Linux systems?
questions.explanation: SSH with strong authentication is the standard safe approach for remote administration.

questions.question: What is the value of testing network connectivity with simple tools first?
answers: It helps isolate whether the issue is network, service, or name resolution
questions.explanation: Simple checks often narrow the problem quickly and efficiently.

questions.question: Which concept best describes a port on a host?
answers: A logical endpoint for network services
questions.explanation: Ports identify network services on a system.

questions.question: Which practice most improves network troubleshooting repeatability?
questions.explanation: Documented steps and evidence improve repeatable troubleshooting.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of hostname-based access over remembering raw IP addresses?
answers: It is easier to manage and remember
questions.explanation: Hostnames are easier for people to manage and can map to changing IPs.
